description:
A small internal container registry is kept on the workstation under
    the path /home/user/container_repo.  
    After rolling out an update you must capture a point-in-time
    inventory of every container image currently present in that
    directory.

    Requirements the automated checker will enforce:

    1. Create the directory /home/user/deployment_logs if it does not yet exist.
    2. Generate a **single file** named
       /home/user/deployment_logs/container_inventory.txt.
    3. The file must contain **only** the filenames (not full paths) of
       every item that already exists inside /home/user/container_repo,
       sorted in **ascending ASCII order** (standard `sort` order).
       • One filename per line.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces.  
       • Terminate the file with **one** newline (␤) after the last item.  
       • Do **not** add extra blank lines anywhere.  
    4. Accomplish the whole task with one compound shell command
       (you are free to use pipes and redirects, but the automated grader
       expects the result to be produced in a single command entered in
       the terminal).

    Example of what the file’s content should look like (actual names
    will depend on what is in the directory at runtime):

        aaa_1.0.sif
        zzz_9.9.sif

    Nothing else should be printed to the screen apart from whatever the
    shell naturally echoes while running your command.  No manual
    editing of the target file is allowed—create it entirely from the
    command you issue.
truth:
Pre-populated directory state (present before the agent starts):

        /home/user/container_repo/backend_2.4.sif
        /home/user/container_repo/frontend_1.8.sif
        /home/user/container_repo/nginx_1.21.sif
        /home/user/container_repo/worker_2.4.sif

    Expected file after successful completion:

        Path: /home/user/deployment_logs/container_inventory.txt
        Permissions: default umask (0644 is acceptable)

        Exact byte-for-byte contents (final newline included):
            backend_2.4.sif\n
            frontend_1.8.sif\n
            nginx_1.21.sif\n
            worker_2.4.sif\n
